job_title: AI Power Performance Validation and Software Engineer
job_description:
• Power performance debug and tuning experience for various workloads, understand workload sensitivities, and tune for most efficient performance.
• Contribute to the definition of firmware requirements and validation strategies to ensure robust coverage of real‑world use cases.
• Experience working with or validating low‑level firmware (e.
• Hands‑on experience with graphics, SoC, or IP‑level firmware used for bring‑up, configuration, or power/clock management.
• Will be required to do FW prototyping, making changes in FW, compile, test and debug for Power/perf prototyping.
• Experience validating systems where firmware plays a critical role in feature enablement and performance tuning.
• Experience collaborating with firmware or embedded software engineers to debug complex system‑level issues.
• , Power management FW, FreeRTOS, Zephyr, Microcontroller FW).

• Develop and debug ATE test patterns and test flowsDefine and execute test plans, characterization, and silicon validationEnsure testability and manufacturability from development through productionAnalyze silicon failures and drive test flow improvementsPerform silicon debug, including performance, yield, and marginality analysisOptimize test flows to improve quality, efficiency, and costAnalyze characterization data to inform design and process improvementsCollaborate with Design and Platform teams to root cause and resolve issues Experience with ATE platforms (e.
• , Verigy 93K) including hardware, software, timing, and pattern developmentKnowledge of test methodologies such as Open/Shorts, Boundary Scan, and connectivity testingFamiliarity with ATE test program development and infrastructure Strong data analysis and presentation skillsProgramming experience in Java, Perl, or C++Unix/Linux proficiencyKnowledge of JTAG and modern processor architectures Understanding of scan compression techniques, failure mechanisms, and debug processesExperience with at-speed (AC scan) development, debug, and characterization Knowledge of modern SRAM operationExperience with SRAM debug, characterization, test, or designFamiliarity with Memory BIST architectures AMD Partner Hub Authorized Distributors AMD University Program Investor Relations Financial Information Board of Directors Governance Documents SEC Filings.
